description: ol-not-a-place (issue #442/F3): a FULLY-parenthesized assignment target is not an assignable place. spec/tooling.md:187 requires rejecting `parenthesized expressions as targets` as a SEMANTIC `ol-not-a-place`, not the blunt parse `ol-bad-token` main produced pre-#442. `parseParenthesized` strips grouping and returns the inner node unchanged, so `(:x)`/`(:x.a)` land as a bare `VarRef`/`Place` byte-identical to the assignable `:x`/`:x.a`; the parser now re-wraps such a target into the zero-segment `PostfixExpression` (the empty-segment shape #407/F7 designed) the checker already renders parenthesized, routing it to the semantic rule. The `=` form (`(:x) = 2`, `(:x.a) = 2`) and the `set … to` form (`set (:x) to 2`) share this recovery. The first line assigns `:x` so this fixture stays focused on `ol-not-a-place` alone, without also tripping `ol-undefined-var` on the same `:x` reads.
